name: AntiLife
description: The black/white reversal of Conway's Game of Life.

name: InverseLife
description: A rule by Jason Rampe, showing similar oscillators and gliders to Conway's Game of Life. The black/white reversal of B36

name: B01245/S01245
description: A strobing rule which makes spearheaders form from chaotic oscillating boundaries.

name: Wickstretcher And The Parasites[1]
description: An explosive strobing rule which forms different wickstretchers of different speeds and sizes, mostly accompanied by a "

name: Oils
description: An exploding rule that forms many wickstrechers, along with other patterns such as a c/16 diagonal spaceship.

name: B026/S1
description: Simulates range-2 wolfram rule 2166637080

name: Invertamaze
description: A strobing rule which makes mazes that invert themselves each generation (SEIZURE WARNING).

name: Neon Blobs
description: A strobing rule in which patterns become neon-like explosive patterns.

name: H-trees
description: An exploding rule where H-shaped branches grow from pattern borders.

name: Fuzz
description: An exploding rule in which solid patterns appear.

name: Gnarl
description: A simple exploding rule by Kellie Evans that forms complex patterns from even a single live cell.

name: Snakeskin
description: A rule in which patterns explode and form snake-like patterns.

name: Solid islands grow amongst static
description: A rule in which patterns grow exponentially and form solid patterns inside static-like patterns.

name: Replicator
description: A rule in which every pattern is a replicator.

name: Fredkin
description: A rule in which, like Replicator, every pattern is a replicator. Also known as "Replicator 2".

name: Feux
description: An explosive rule where patterns turn into replicators and flash in and out of existence.

name: Forrest of Ls
description: An explosive rule where the insides of patterns slowy stablize into still formations.

name: Seeds
description: An exploding rule by Brian Silverman in which every cell dies in every generation. It has many simple orthogonal spacesh

name: Live Free or Die
description: An exploding rule in which only cells with no neighbors survive. It has many spaceships, puffers, and oscillators, some

name: B2/S13
description: An exploding rule, one of the few B2 life-like rules that has a replicator.

name: B2/S2345
description: An exploding rule, with still lifes that may form indestructible barriers to growth, which can be used as casing for sin

name: B2/S23456
description: Similar to B2/S2345[3]

name: B2/S2345678
description: Similar to B2/S2345[4]

name: Serviettes[5]
description: An exploding rule in which every cell dies in every generation (like Seeds). This rule is of interest because of the fab

name: B25/S4
description: An exploding rule, it has a one-dimensional asymmetric replicator.

name: Iceballs
description: Small masses of solid living cells flicker in and out of existence. Some reach a critical mass and begin to slowly grow.

name: Life without death
description: An expanding rule that produces complex flakes, featuring dense wickstretchers named "ladders". Also known as "Flakes" o

name: DotLife
description: An exploding rule closely related to Conway's Life. The B-heptomino is a common infinite growth pattern in this rule, th

name: Star Trek
description: A rule with several spaceships of various speeds.

name: Flock
description: Patterns tend to quickly settle into dominoes, duoplets and period 2 oscillators. There is a common period 14 shuttle os

name: Mazectric
description: An expanding rule that crystalizes to form maze-like designs that tend to be straighter (ie. have longer "halls") than t

name: Maze
description: An expanding rule that crystalizes to form maze-like designs.

name: Magnezones
description: An explosive rule that is liquid with four types of crystals appearing in the liquid.

name: Corrosion of Conformity
description: A rule similar to Mazectric but without S3. A slow burn from almost any starting pattern, resulting in a rusting away of

name: B3/S2
description: This rule has 6 small still lifes, the tub, the hive, the aircraft carrier, the loaf, the mango, and the pond. However,

name: Conway's Life
description: A chaotic rule that is by far the most well-known and well-studied. It exhibits highly complex behavior.

name: EightLife
description: Also known as "Pulsar Life".

name: Shoots and Roots
description: Shoots are slow growing, well-ordered patterns, while Roots grow faster than shoots, and explosive as they grow.

name: Coral
description: An exploding rule in which patterns grow slowly and form coral-like textures.

name: 3-4 Life
description: An exploding rule that was initially thought to be a stable alternative to Conway's Life, until computer simulation foun

name: Never happy
description: An explosive rule where patterns slowly form diamond-like shapes and slowly explode.

name: Assimilation
description: A very stable rule that forms permanent diamond-shaped patterns with partially filled interiors. Like in 2×2, patterns m

name: Long Life
description: A stable rule that gets its name from the fact that it has many simple extremely high period oscillators.

name: Spiral and polygonal growth
description: A rule with "plow" structures that move along an edge of a polygon, increasing its size (turning corners and thickening

name: Gems
description: An exploding rule with many smaller high-period oscillators and a c/5648 spaceship.

name: Gems Minor
description: An exploding rule with many smaller high-period oscillators and a c/2068 spaceship.

name: Grounded Life
description: A stable rule "one outer-totalistic condition away from Life", where neither of the standard spaceships works, and the m

name: Land Rush
description: Expanding chaos organizing itself into plowed fields.

name: Eppstein
description: An exploding rule explored by Dean Hickerson and David Eppstein.[8]

name: Bugs[6]
description: An explosive rule with many high density spaceships.

name: Cheerios
description: An explosive rule where patterns explode while "cheerios" form in the chaotic mess.

name: Holstein[9]
description: A self-complementary rule. Black regions are surrounded by froth and tend to eventually collapse starting from the corne

name: Diamoeba
description: A chaotic pattern that forms large diamonds with chaotically oscillating boundaries. Known to have quadratically-growing

name: Amoeba
description: A chaotic rule that is well balanced between life and death; it forms patterns with chaotic interiors and wildly moving

name: Pseudo Life
description: A chaotic rule with evolution that resembles Conway's Life, but few patterns from Life work in this rule because the gli

name: Geology
description: A very well-balanced and chaotic rule that forms large "continents" of live cells surrounded by chaos at a roughly 50% d

name: 2×2
description: A chaotic rule with many simple still lifes, oscillators and spaceships. Its name comes from the fact that it sends patt

name: HighLife
description: A chaotic rule very similar to Conway's Life that is of interest because it has a simple replicator.

name: Land Rush 2
description: An exploding rule that got confused with Land Rush, likely due to a typo.  It does contain a period 4 orthogonal c/2 shi

name: Virus
description: An exploding rule where the T-tetromino is a blinker puffer, appropriate because it evolves into traffic light in Life,

name: sqrt replicator rule
description: Name comes from a dirty orthogonal replicator, formerly known as the logarithmic replicator rule.

name: DrighLife
description: A mix between DryLife and HighLife

name: Castles
description: A rule where patterns almost explode into castle-like stable patterns.

name: B3678/S23
description: Black/white reversal of InverseLife.

name: Stains
description: A stable rule in which most patterns tend to "fill in" bounded regions. Most nearby rules (such as coagulations) tend to

name: Day & Night
description: A stable rule that is symmetric under on-off reversal. Many patterns exhibiting highly complex behavior have been found

name: B368/S12578
description: An exploding 2x2-like rule with a small 4c/13o replicator, of which guns and W110 unit cells had been constructed [10]

name: Life SkyHigh
description: A rule that is similar to HighLife but adds a B8 and S6 to the rulestring, hence, the name "SkyHigh".

name: LowDeath
description: HighLife's replicator works in this rule, albeit with a different evolution sequence due to the result of B38/S23's "ped

name: Morley
description: A rule in which random patterns tend to stabilize extremely quickly. Has a very common slow-moving spaceship and slow-mo

name: DryLife without Death
description: Similar to Life without death, but patterns tend to grow quadratically instead of forming 'ladders'.

name: Mazectric with Mice
description: Some "mice" run back and forth in the halls of the maze.

name: Maze with Mice
description: Similar to B37/S1234, some "mice" run back and forth in the continually-stretching halls of the maze.

name: DryLife
description: An exploding rule closely related to Conway's Life, named after the fact that standard spaceships bigger than the glider

name: Plow World
description: An explosive rule, some of the patterns can turn into wickstretchers.

name: Coagulations
description: An exploding rule in which patterns tend to expand forever, producing a thick "goo" as it does so. Surprisingly, Coagula

name: Pedestrian Flock
description: A mix between Pedestrian Life and Flock.

name: Pedestrian Life
description: A close Life variant with a number of distinctive natural growth patterns and (5,2)c/190 oblique spaceships.

name: HoneyLife
description: A mix between Pedestrian Life and EightLife.

name: Oscillators Rule[11]
description: Random patterns shrink to tiny oscillators, with periods anywhere between 1 ("still lifes") and 16. Larger periods are r

name: Walled cities
description: A stable rule by David Macfarlane that forms centers of pseudo-random activity separated by walls.

name: Vote 4/5[12]
description: A modification of the standard Gérard Vichniac voting rule, also known as "Anneal", used as a model for majority voting.

name: Rings 'n' Slugs
description: A rule in which patterns stabilize into "Rings" and "Slugs".

name: Vote[12]
description: Standard Gérard Vichniac voting rule, also known as "Majority", used as a model for majority voting.
